tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mildly elevated creatinine, low calcium, and high chloride, suggesting possible renal impairment or dehydration. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, raising concern for renal dysfunction and possible metabolic derangement. Low HbA1c and absence of infection markers (nitrite, leukocytes) are noted. Recommend further evaluation for renal function, metabolic status, and possible underlying causes.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
